question: Which statement about process instance tags is correct?

options.text: Tags can be added, changed, or removed after the process instance has been created.
A process instance accepts up to 10 unique tags and tags are immutable after creation.
Tags are case-insensitive and may begin with a digit.
Tags are stored as process variables and are visible in Operate and Tasklist.

a.text: The docs explicitly state tags are immutable after creation and cannot be added, changed, or removed once the instance exists.

b.text: The docs specify a maximum of 10 unique tags per process instance and that tags cannot be modified after creation.

c.text: Tags are case-sensitive and the format regex requires the first character to be a letter (A–Z or a–z), not a digit.

d.text: Tags are API/client-only metadata; they are not shown in Operate or Tasklist and are distinct from process variables.

explanation: Tags are lightweight, immutable, case-sensitive labels attached at instance creation. Each instance accepts up to 10 unique tags following the format regex `^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_\-:.]{0,99}$`, and they are not surfaced in Operate or Tasklist.
